title: Trump in Defeat
description: The president went to war triumphant and will likely leave greatly weakened.
author: Jonathan Lemire

title: Trump Does Not Understand the War He Lost
description: The president’s comments at the G7 summit revealed that he doesn’t understand the war he started—or the words that come out of his own mouth.
author: Tom Nichols

title: The Doha Connection
description: Trump’s off-ramp from war with Iran runs through Qatar.
author: Shane Harris

title: Oil Prices Might Not Go Back to Normal Anytime Soon
description: There’s a big difference between reopening the Strait of Hormuz on paper and actually resuming the flow of oil through it.
author: Rogé Karma

title: Assume You Will Be Hacked
description: AI is enabling a deluge of cyberattacks the likes of which we’ve never seen before.
author: Matteo Wong

title: Why July 4 Turned Into a Trump Rally
description: The president has never accepted that the head of state and the leader of the Republican Party are separate roles.
author: Jonathan Chait

title: The Butterfly Longevity Diet
description: Researchers aren’t sure why Heliconius butterflies live so long, but pollen may be part of the answer.
author: Katherine J. Wu

title: The Betrayal of the Iranian People
description: The world has not reckoned with the violence visited on Iranians in 2026.
author: Laura Secor

title: The Cult of Delayed Gratification Is a Lie
description: The sensory enchantment of everyday life can upend the bland monotony of our overly optimized, anodyne world.
author: Ian Bogost

title: AtlanticTrivia: Patriotic Music
description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
author: Drew Goins

title: The Humanities Were Never Neutral
description: Readers respond to our March 2026 issue.

title: Finally, an Action Movie About Washington’s French and Indian War Years
description: Young Washington gives us pre-Revolutionary George, bristling with awkwardness and ambition.
author: James Parker

title: The U.S. Had No Choice but Diplomacy—Yet Again
description: The new pact between the U.S. and Iran seeks to rewind the clock to the day before the war. ​​​​​​
author: Nancy A. Youssef,Russell Berman,  andVivian Salama

title: The White House Is Ratcheting Up Its War Against Anthropic
description: This is how America loses the AI race.
author: Matteo Wong

title: There’s a Name for the People Who Drain You
description: “Hasslers” make life more difficult—and we can’t escape them.
author: Olga Khazan

title: The Warner Bros.–Paramount Merger Isn’t Hollywood’s Biggest Problem
description: Regardless of what happens between the studios, the film industry “is still in trouble,” a historian says.
author: Shirley Li

title: The Unpopular Truth About World Cup Ticket Prices
description: When seats cost too little, ticket speculators reap the benefits.
author: Judd Kessler

title: The Conversions of J. D. Vance
description: Politically and spiritually, the vice president has been on a journey.
author: Vivian Salama

title: The Theory That Explains Trump’s UFC Fight
description: By staging a “spectacle of excess” on the White House lawn, the president expressed the violent essence of his worldview.
author: Gal Beckerman

title: Nathaniel Hawthorne’s American Horror Story
description: The author wrote a tale that challenged the nation’s founding myths. Then it disappeared.
author: John Swansburg
